contrastMetadata.transferBoundary: Transfer when the task changes between structural matching or enclosure and a nearest qualifying value or boundary; the retained state and correctness invariant must change with the output.

authoringProvenance.author: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Pro — candidate authoring assistant; human review required

items.prompt: A design review covers Mixed brackets. Classify balanced delimiters as nested-structure validation. Which statement gives the decisive justification?

options.text: A closer must match the most recent compatible opener.
The task is nearest greater because a closer appears later.
Both tasks use the same invariant because both can be implemented with a stack; only the token names differ.
Choose the data structure from the surface domain alone, without considering whether the output is structural closure or a per-element boundary.

feedback.reason: A closer must match the most recent compatible opener. This is the decisive boundary for the stated output and retained-state semantics.
feedback.details: The item is decided by structural closure versus nearest qualifying boundary resolution, not by a surface feature. A closer must match the most recent compatible opener. The tempting claim—The task is nearest greater because a closer appears later.—fails because it does not establish the semantic invariant that makes removal or retention safe. Transfer when the task changes between structural matching or enclosure and a nearest qualifying value or boundary; the retained state and correctness invariant must change with the output.

items.prompt: Select all correct contrasts between nested-structure validation and nearest-boundary search.
constraints: Select all and only the statements that remain valid under the exact output and tie policy.
Each selected statement must be justified by retained-state semantics or a correctness/cost argument.

options.text: Nearest-boundary search commonly produces a separate answer for many input positions.
The retained entry means unmatched structure in one case and unresolved ordered candidate in the other.
Both classes always return a single boolean result.
Both classes retain values solely to keep them globally sorted.
Nested validation resolves structural openers or frames, often producing one aggregate validity or parsed result.

feedback.reason: The accepted set jointly establishes the decisive boundary: Nested tasks resolve structures; nearest-boundary tasks usually produce per-element answers.
feedback.details: The stack category follows from the proof obligation. Nested tasks resolve structures; nearest-boundary tasks usually produce per-element answers. Names, indices, comparisons, loop shape, or one trace snapshot cannot replace that proof. In this case, the stated misconception—Both problems retain exactly the same semantic object.—would permit transitions that are not justified by the requested result. Transfer when the task changes between structural matching or enclosure and a nearest qualifying value or boundary; the retained state and correctness invariant must change with the output.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.false_claim_1: This claim is not sufficient or is false under the stated contract. Nested tasks resolve structures; nearest-boundary tasks usually produce per-element answers.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.false_claim_2: This claim is not sufficient or is false under the stated contract. Nested tasks resolve structures; nearest-boundary tasks usually produce per-element answers.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.required_fact_1: This fact is required because it contributes to the complete decision boundary: Nested tasks resolve structures; nearest-boundary tasks usually produce per-element answers.

items.prompt: Order one delimiter-match transition when a closing token arrives.
constraints: Order the steps by semantic dependency, not by the order in which they are listed.
The canonical scoring contract awards preserved adjacent relations.

elements.text: Verify that an unmatched opener exists on the stack.
Inspect the most recent unmatched opener at the top.
Verify that its type matches the closing token.
Pop the matched opener and continue with the remaining structural context.

feedback.reason: The sequence follows the dependency expressed by the decisive boundary: Validate closer against top, then pop the matched opener; no value-order repair occurs.
feedback.details: Apply the contract before choosing the representation. Validate closer against top, then pop the matched opener; no value-order repair occurs. This preserves the relevant state and prevents premature deletion or unnecessary ordering. The alternative heuristic—Pop all openers that compare below the closer.—is too broad and would classify unrelated stack algorithms the same way. Transfer when the task changes between structural matching or enclosure and a nearest qualifying value or boundary; the retained state and correctness invariant must change with the output. Reversing or separating a required adjacent relation either uses state before it is valid, loses provenance before writing an answer, or mutates the frontier before the invariant has been restored.

items.prompt: Order one next-greater transition when the current value is larger than several retained candidates.
constraints: Order the steps by semantic dependency, not by the order in which they are listed.
The canonical scoring contract awards preserved adjacent relations.

elements.text: Push the current item as unresolved after all applicable candidates are finalized.
Compare the current value with the top unresolved candidate.
Pop a smaller candidate under the strict relation.
Record the current value or index as that candidate’s next-greater answer.
Repeat while another smaller candidate remains on top.

feedback.reason: The sequence follows the dependency expressed by the decisive boundary: Pop and answer all smaller top candidates, then push current as unresolved.
feedback.details: The item is decided by structural closure versus nearest qualifying boundary resolution, not by a surface feature. Pop and answer all smaller top candidates, then push current as unresolved. The tempting claim—Match current to only one top as in nesting.—fails because it does not establish the semantic invariant that makes removal or retention safe. Transfer when the task changes between structural matching or enclosure and a nearest qualifying value or boundary; the retained state and correctness invariant must change with the output. Reversing or separating a required adjacent relation either uses state before it is valid, loses provenance before writing an answer, or mutates the frontier before the invariant has been restored.
